vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1132: TLDR — fix broken status reporting + expose to work agents

plan.title: TLDR: fix broken status reporting + expose to work agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

references.label: Prior PAN-1132 fix (daemon pidfile + pan-tldr skill + roles/* TLDR sections)

narratives.Problem: Three TLDR concerns: (1) `pan admin tldr status` reported running daemons as stopped because the CLI wrote its own state file with the CLI process's PID as a fallback, then reaped it on next read; (2) the work-agent-facing `pan-tldr` skill was removed in 0.7.0's legacy-skill purge and actively deleted by `pan sync`; (3) only `cloister/prompts/work.md` had a TLDR guidance block — review/test/planning/resume-work prompts were silent on TLDR. Concerns #1 and #2 were already addressed by commit ab8cd6f70 on main, and that commit also added unconditional TLDR sections to `roles/plan.md`, `roles/test.md`, and the four `roles/review-*.md` sub-roles. What remains is concern #3 literally: the four cloister prompt templates listed in the issue body still lack the `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` conditional block.
narratives.Proposal: Add a `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` conditional block (same shape as the existing one in `cloister/prompts/work.md`) to each of `cloister/prompts/planning.md`, `cloister/prompts/review.md`, `cloister/prompts/test.md`, and `cloister/prompts/resume-work.md`. Add `TLDR_AVAILABLE` to the `optional:` frontmatter of each so `renderPrompt` permits the variable. Wire `spawn-planning-session.ts` to pass `TLDR_AVAILABLE: existsSync(join(workspacePath, '.venv'))` — the planning prompt is the only one of the four with an active production render site. Finish with an end-to-end verification bead that confirms all three issue criteria still hold.

autoDecisions.summary: Apply the {{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}} block to all four cloister prompts listed in the issue body, including the three that no production code currently renders (review.md, test.md, resume-work.md).
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body's Fix #3 lists all four explicitly. The block is a no-op when TLDR_AVAILABLE is unset, so adding it has zero runtime cost and brings the templates in line with the issue's literal request. The templates are still exercised by `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/prompts.test.ts`, so they are not pure dead code. If/when they are revived they will be TLDR-aware from day one.

autoDecisions.summary: Do NOT delete the unused review/test/resume-work cloister prompt files in this issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: Their deletion would require updating prompts.test.ts assertions and is independent of the TLDR exposure concern. Treat as a separate cleanup if needed.

autoDecisions.summary: Skip changes to roles/review.md (the synthesis role).
autoDecisions.rationale: The role file at roles/review.md is the convoy-synthesis agent — it polls reviewer output files instead of doing fresh code exploration. The heavy-reading sub-roles (roles/review-{security,correctness,performance,requirements}.md) already have TLDR sections from commit ab8cd6f70.

Commit ab8cd6f70 (on main) already replaced the broken state-file machinery with reads from `<workspace>/.tldr/daemon.pid` and restored the `pan-tldr` skill. Verification of this state was performed during planning (workspace daemon at PID 291042 reports running/healthy via `pan admin tldr status`). Touching these files again would be wasted effort and risks regression.

items.title: Add {{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}} block to cloister/prompts/planning.md and wire spawn-planning-session.ts

narrative.Action: Add `TLDR_AVAILABLE` to the `optional:` frontmatter list in `src/lib/cloister/prompts/planning.md`. Insert a planning-flavored `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block — short, mirroring the structure of the existing block in `cloister/prompts/work.md` (tldr_context / tldr_structure / tldr_semantic / tldr_calls / tldr_impact) but framed for planning's broad-exploration workflow. Update `src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts` (the only production caller of `renderPrompt({ name: 'planning' })` at line 281) to pass `TLDR_AVAILABLE: existsSync(join(workspacePath, '.venv'))` in the `vars` object. The `existsSync` and `join` imports already exist in that file.

subItems.title: src/lib/cloister/prompts/planning.md has `TLDR_AVAILABLE` in its `optional:` frontmatter list

planning.md body contains a `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block that names the five TLDR MCP tools (tldr_context, tldr_structure, tldr_semantic, tldr_calls, tldr_impact) and tells the planning agent to prefer summaries over full Reads during discovery
src/lib/planning/spawn-planning-session.ts passes `TLDR_AVAILABLE: existsSync(join(workspacePath, '.venv'))` in the renderPrompt call at the `name: 'planning'` site

Rendering planning.md with `TLDR_AVAILABLE: true` includes the TLDR section; rendering with the variable absent or `false` produces no TLDR section (verified by a new or updated test in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/prompts.test.ts, or by an inline assertion-style snippet in the existing MEMORY_CONTEXT test)
`npm run typecheck` and the prompts.test.ts test file pass after the change

narrative.Action: Add `TLDR_AVAILABLE` to the `optional:` frontmatter list in `src/lib/cloister/prompts/review.md`. Insert a review-flavored `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block that emphasizes TLDR for navigating the diff's surrounding context (tldr_context, tldr_calls, tldr_impact, tldr_semantic). No production render site needs wiring — this template is exercised only by `prompts.test.ts` today; the conditional block is a no-op when the var is unset.

subItems.title: review.md has `TLDR_AVAILABLE` in its `optional:` frontmatter list
review.md body contains a `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block framed for code-review usage (context for changed files, caller analysis, semantic search for related code)
Existing prompts.test.ts assertion that renders review.md without TLDR_AVAILABLE still passes (no required-var error, no unwanted output)

narrative.Action: Add `TLDR_AVAILABLE` to the `optional:` frontmatter list in `src/lib/cloister/prompts/test.md`. Insert a test-flavored `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block emphasizing TLDR for diagnosing test failures (tldr_context for fixtures/helpers, tldr_calls/tldr_impact for tracing failing functions, tldr_semantic for finding where a behavior is implemented when an AC fails).

subItems.title: test.md has `TLDR_AVAILABLE` in its `optional:` frontmatter list
test.md body contains a `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block framed for test-failure diagnosis
Existing prompts.test.ts assertion that renders test.md without TLDR_AVAILABLE still passes

narrative.Action: Add `TLDR_AVAILABLE` to the `optional:` frontmatter list in `src/lib/cloister/prompts/resume-work.md`. Insert a resume-work-flavored `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block emphasizing TLDR for re-orienting on a partial implementation (tldr_context on the in-flight files to recover structure without re-reading the whole file).

subItems.title: resume-work.md has `TLDR_AVAILABLE` in its `optional:` frontmatter list
resume-work.md body contains a `{{#TLDR_AVAILABLE}}…{{/TLDR_AVAILABLE}}` block framed for re-orienting on partial work
renderPrompt({ name: 'resume-work', vars: {} }) — or the equivalent existing test pattern — still succeeds with the new optional var

items.title: End-to-end verification of all three PAN-1132 issue criteria

narrative.Action: Run the three verification commands listed in the issue body and document outcomes in the PR description (or as a comment on PAN-1132 if there is no PR). This is a verification-only bead — no code changes.

subItems.title: `pan admin tldr status` correctly reports the workspace daemon as running with a real PID (not the CLI's PID), and after a daemon stop reports stopped. State must survive CLI exit (run the command, exit, run it again — same result).
After `pan sync`, `~/.claude/skills/pan-tldr/SKILL.md` exists and is not deleted on a subsequent `pan sync` (i.e. pan-tldr is NOT in LEGACY_SKILL_NAMES).

All four cloister prompts (planning, review, test, resume-work) render a TLDR section when `TLDR_AVAILABLE=true` and render no TLDR section when the variable is unset. work.md already does this — confirm no regression.

All seven role files that touch code-reading workflows (roles/plan.md, roles/work.md, roles/test.md, roles/review-security.md, roles/review-correctness.md, roles/review-performance.md, roles/review-requirements.md) still contain their TLDR sections from commit ab8cd6f70 — regression check.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` (root + frontend) all pass.
